title: FEATURE REQUEST - 2FA to open application
author: pir8radio
body_snippet: Encryption of the connections file is great, but I would feel even safer if I could enable 2fa to even open the application,  I don't think it needs a user/pass to login, just a function to enable something like using google authenticator or Authy,  to 2fa into the app..  I do not save login info within mRemoteNG today because if i loose my laptop, its pretty easy to get into windows.    then they have the key to my servers.

iterations.description: Bulk verified: commit dd5f4dc7 exists (HIGH confidence)

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: that is palned for user profiles

comments.author: My1
comments.snippet:
the biggest issue tho is this needs to be made in a way that isnt pure snake oil.

TOTP (as in the algo that Google Authenticator etc generally use) only works because it is used to authenticate at remote systems which can deny giving you any data in the first place.

However with mRem your keys/passwords etc, if stored in there, are local, this means the only way to properly secure them is encryption. an Authentication-only approach would not really work as an attacker could just bypass the...

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet:
> the store can already be encrypted right? I just want a simple 2FA to decrypt that file. For google authenticator and Authy no internet is needed let alone any encryption keys sent over the net. Its based on time and shared keys.

It is correct that the TOTP protocol does not require any Internet. The problem is (like @My1 statet) that you are not connecting to a backend that validates the second factor, but you are on that backend. This means an attacker that has access to your system and t...

comments.author: My1
comments.snippet:
exactly. the encryption of that file can only use your password, or some otherwise static key to actually encrypt that file, as you cannot have that thing change until you go and decrypt it again.

things like TOTP I generally call a "data valve" you can use it to deny access but that really only works when the attacker is on the other side of that valve.

in Fact I have written a big thing about this entire thing when a kinda reputable thing like Steganos went and implemented TOTP
https://...

So logical link will be looks as that:
App -> User (local login, or network login) + 2FA -> User profile (where is always default user profile if user not ab...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `dd5f4dc7` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** FEATURE REQUEST - 2FA to open application

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork builds entir...

notes:
AI triage (claude): Valid security enhancement request to add optional 2FA (TOTP) at application startup, protecting stored credentials from unauthorized access on shared workstations.
Approach: Add optional TOTP-based 2FA setting in app options; on startup, if enabled, show authentication dialog requiring TOTP code before loading connection tree and credentials
